perturbation.original_text:
As consideration for Celebrity's services under this Agreement, HDS agrees as follows:
A. In-kind Payment: HDS will provide Celebrity with a one (1) year complimentary supply (i.e., at least 365 caplets) of Clotamin product during the Term.
perturbation.changed_text:
As consideration for Celebrity's services under this Agreement, HDS agrees as follows:
A. In-kind Payment: HDS may, at its sole discretion, provide Celebrity with a complimentary supply of Clotamin product during the Term. The amount and duration of such supply are subject to HDS's internal policies and availability.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that HDS 'will provide' a one-year supply of Clotamin. The modified text changes this to 'may, at its sole discretion, provide,' which introduces uncertainty about whether Celebrity will actually receive the product. This contradicts Section 5B(1), where the Cash Payment is considered an obligation, and creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5 itself, between A and B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Consideration. As consideration for Celebrity's services under this Agreement, HDS agrees as follows: A. In-kind Payment: HDS may, at its sole discretion, provide Celebrity with a complimentary supply of Clotamin product during the Term. The amount and duration of such supply are subject to HDS's internal policies and availability. B. Cash Payment: (1) HDS will provide payment of Fifty Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($50,000) made payable to Celebrity's agent. CSE, as follows $10,000 Within ten (10) days of the parties execution of this Agreement $15,000 Within 60 days of the parties execution of this agreement $25,000* Ten (10) days prior to the Production Session [*Should Company decide not to conduct the Production Session, then the $25,000 allocated for said Production Session ("Production Fee") will not be owed to Celebrity and notice should be given to CSE as soon as reasonably known by Company if Company does not intend to conduct the Production Session. However, if Company schedules the Production Session, then said Production Fee is non-refundable regardless if the Production Session occurs. ] (a) CSE, shall invoice HDS for these fees and HDS shall pay such invoice within ten (10) business days following receipt of the CSE invoice. (b) Payments shall be made to CSE and delivered to 600 Galleria Parkway, Suite 1900, Atlanta, Georgia 30339. (2) If HDS desires to add an additional market or state to the Markets for the Commercial to air during the Term, then HOS will pay Celebrity Five Thousand and NO/100 Dollars ($5,000) for said additional state/market, and the parties will amend the Agreement to add the additional state/market accordingly.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5A stated that HDS 'will provide' a one-year supply, creating a definite obligation. The changed text alters this to 'may, at its sole discretion, provide,' transforming it into an option. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the explicit cash payment obligation in Section 5B(1), creating an in-text contradiction within Section 5 itself. While Section 5B(1) allows for the Production Fee to be withheld if a session is not conducted (and requires notice), the modified Section 5A introduces ambiguity in the In-kind Payment and creates inconsistency. This inconsistency makes it unclear whether Celebrity is entitled to any payment at all in kind.

perturbation.original_text: All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases shall be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. Said written approval must be given within five (5) business days of CSE's receipt or said usage shall be deemed unapproved.
perturbation.changed_text: All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases may, at HDS's discretion, be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. HDS shall make reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, but the ultimate decision on usage rests solely with HDS.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that HDS's use of Celebrity Attributes is 'subject to the prior written approval' of Celebrity. This gives Celebrity control over how their image is used. The modified text changes this to 'may be subject to' approval, and states that 'the ultimate decision on usage rests solely with HDS.' This creates an in-text contradiction within section 9. It also contradicts the intended purpose of the approval process, as originally outlined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Review, Approval, and Ownership of Advertising. All HDS' uses of Celebrity Attributes in connection with the Commercial and/or press releases may, at HDS's discretion, be subject to the prior written approval of Celebrity via his agent, CSE. HDS shall make reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, but the ultimate decision on usage rests solely with HDS. Any such usage featuring Celebrity in the Commercial and/or press releases shall be and remain the property of HDS; however, HDS shall have the right to use said Commercial and/or press releases solely as outlined in Paragraph 3 and only during the Term. Celebrity may use said materials in whole or in part solely for the purpose of presenting Celebrity's work in Celebrity's personal portfolio, website or otherwise and/or on Celebrity's agent's website. Such usage may not be sold or transferred.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction within the document. The original text requires Celebrity's prior written approval for HDS's use of Celebrity Attributes, giving Celebrity control. The changed text, which is also the 'Contradicted Text,' states that HDS's use 'may be subject to' approval and that 'the ultimate decision on usage rests solely with HDS.' This directly conflicts with the original approval process, creating an in-text contradiction because the same clause has conflicting implications due to the modification. The change directly breaks compatibility because the approval, which used to be mandatory, is now at HDS's discretion.

perturbation.original_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity in the event Celebrity fails to perform the duties set forth in Paragraph 4 hereof or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement; however, such termination will require a payment of $10,000 to Celebrity, regardless of the reason for termination. Termination may occur at any time with ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that HDS can terminate if Celebrity fails to perform duties, relieving HDS of further consideration. The modified version allows HDS to terminate at any time, but requires a $10,000 payment even if Celebrity is in breach. This contradicts the original intent of section 10A where non-performance by celebrity voids any further consideration due to the celebrity. This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the consequences of termination for HDS, regardless of celebrity performance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. HDS shall have the right to terminate this Agreement; however, such termination will require a payment of $10,000 to Celebrity, regardless of the reason for termination. Termination may occur at any time with ten (10) days prior written notice to Celebrity. Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows HDS to terminate the agreement at any time with a $10,000 payment, regardless of the reason. The contradicted text also says that HDS can terminate the agreement at any time with a $10,000 payment, regardless of the reason. However, the contradicted text contains an additional sentence that says 'Such termination shall relieve HDS of its obligation to provide any further consideration pursuant to this Agreement.'. This sentence in the contradicted text contradicts the previous sentence by implying HDS is not obligated to pay $10,000 in the event of a termination where Celebrity is in breach. The addition of the payment requirement in the changed text directly conflicts with the circumstances in which HDS would be relieved of further consideration according to the original text and the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction that would cause confusion over the terms of termination.
